verdict_reasoning: ITPR3 is a canonical endoplasmic reticulum calcium channel localized to the ER membrane, with a 6-transmembrane architecture, large cytoplasmic domain, and regulatory domains all confined to the ER-lumen and cytoplasm. No cell-surface-directed therapeutics, no ectodomain shedding, no documented PM minority pool or cell-state-induced surface display, no covalent TM-partner tethering, and no evidence for lysosomal exocytosis or tissue-restricted surface display. The protein body has no mechanism to reach the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane.
